# Team News: Argentina vs. Egypt injury, suspension list, predicted XIs | World Cup 2026

By Ben Knapton| 06 Jul 2026 17:00

© Imago / JNA Press

Argentina's next World Cup 2026 examination sees the reigning champions take on Egypt in Tuesday's last-16 match at the Mercedes-Benz Stadium.

Lionel Scaloni's men escaped one of the biggest shocks in World Cup history in the last 32, edging out Cape Verde 3-2 in extra time, while the Pharaohs bested Australia on penalties.

Here, Sports Mole rounds up the team news for both nations.

---

## ARGENTINA vs. EGYPT

https://www.sportsmole.co.uk/football/world-cup/argentina-vs-egypt_game_249591.html

## ARGENTINA

Out: None

Doubtful: Nicolas Gonzalez(ankle), Facundo Medina(cramp)

Sports Mole's predicted XI: E. Martinez; Molina, Romero, L. Martinez, Medina; De Paul, Fernandez, Mac Allister; Messi, Martinez, Almada

## EGYPT

Out: None

Doubtful: Karim Hafez (muscle), Mohamed Abdelmonem (ankle), Ahmed Fatouh (thigh)

Sports Mole's predicted XI: Shobeir; Hany, Fathy, Ibrahim, Rabia; Ashour,

news_items.title: Argentina vs. Egypt—World Cup: Preview, Predictions and Lineups
news_items.text:
Argentina vs. Egypt—World Cup: Preview, Predictions and Lineups

Argentina continues its World Cup title defense when facing Egypt in the round of 16 at Mercedes-Benz Stadium on Tuesday.

A date with one of Switzerland or Colombia is the prize in the quarterfinal, but La Albiceleste can ill-afford any complacency in Atlanta having already been given an almighty scare in the knockout phase. Cabo Verde stunned Lionel Scaloni and his players, forcing extra time and making Argentina sweat in its eventual 3–2 victory. Despite a very favorable fixture list, there are no easy matches.

It was the first time Lionel Messi and Co. have been truly tested this summer, with routine wins over Algeria, Austria and Jordan in the group stage perhaps lulling them into a false sense of security. Egypt will seek to follow the blueprint laid out by Cabo Verde, although it was also pushed to extra time and eventually penalties in its own round of 32 clash with Australia.

The Pharaohs remain unbeaten at the tournament and are looking to make a first-ever quarterfinal, but the team knows perfection will be required to overcome a wily South American adversary. Egypt lost its only previous meeting with Arg
highlights: Scaloni has several fitness concerns ahead of Tuesday's match. Nicolás González is a doubt with an ankle injury, while fullback duo Nahuel ...

news_items.title: Ibrahim Hassan announces the absence of two Egyptian national team players from the World Cup match against Argentina
news_items.author: Ahmed Khairy
news_items.text:
Ibrahim Hassan announces the absence of two Egyptian national team players from the World Cup match against Argentina

Egypt national team (Photo/Archive)

SHARE

Cairo, Egypt – Ibrahim Hassan, the director of the Egyptian national team, revealed the latest developments regarding the injured players’ condition before the anticipated match against Argentina in the round of 16 of the 2026 World Cup. He confirmed that the coaching staff will only be missing two players due to injury.

The Egyptian national team will face Argentina on Tuesday evening in the round of 16 of the World Cup. In this match, the Pharaohs will be looking to continue their historic run in the tournament.

Ibrahim Hassan said in televised remarks on Sada El Balad channel: “We will not suffer from absences against Argentina except for Ahmed Fatouh and Mohamed Abdel Moneim. Their chances of playing in the match seem difficult due to injury”.

The team manager spoke about Mohamed Salah, praising the Pharaohs’ captain’s performances with the national team. He said, “Mohamed Salah appears in a completely different light when he joins the national team camp, and he delivers outstanding performances under Hossam Hassan

# Team News: Argentina vs. Egypt injury, suspension list, predicted XIs | World Cup 2026

By Ben Knapton| 06 Jul 2026 17:00

© Imago / JNA Press

Argentina's next World Cup 2026 examination sees the reigning champions take on Egypt in Tuesday's last-16 match at the Mercedes-Benz Stadium.

Lionel Scaloni's men escaped one of the biggest shocks in World Cup history in the last 32, edging out Cape Verde 3-2 in extra time, while the Pharaohs bested Australia on penalties.

Here, Sports Mole rounds up the team news for both nations.

---

## ARGENTINA vs. EGYPT

https://www.sportsmole.co.uk/football/world-cup/argentina-vs-egypt_game_249591.html

Out: None

Doubtful: Nicolas Gonzalez(ankle), Facundo Medina(cramp)

Sports Mole's predicted XI: E. Martinez; Molina, Romero, L. Martinez, Medina; De Paul, Fernandez, Mac Allister; Messi, Martinez, Almada

## EGYPT

Out: None

Doubtful: Karim Hafez (muscle), Mohamed Abdelmonem (ankle), Ahmed Fatouh (thigh)

Sports Mole's predicted XI: Shobeir; Hany, Fathy, Ibrahim, Rabia; Ashour, "}

{"title": "Argentina vs. Egypt—World Cup: Preview, Predictions and Lineups", "url": "https://www.si.com/soccer/argentina-vs-egypt-world-cup-preview-predictions-lineups-7-7-26", "publishedDate": "2026-07-07T01:00:00.000Z", "author": null, "text": "Argentina vs. Egypt—World Cup: Preview, Predictions and Lineups

Argentina continues its World Cup title defense when facing Egypt in the round of 16 at Mercedes-Benz Stadium on Tuesday.

A date with one of Switzerland or Colombia is the prize in the quarterfinal, but La Albiceleste can ill-afford any complacency in Atlanta having already been given an almighty scare in the knockout phase. Cabo Verde stunned Lionel Scaloni and his players, forcing extra time and making Argentina sweat in its eventual 3–2 victory. Despite a very favorable fixture list, there are no easy matches.

It was the first time Lionel Messi and Co. have been truly tested this summer, with routine wins over Algeria, Austria and Jordan in the group stage perhaps lulling them into a false sense of security. Egypt will seek to follow the blueprint laid out by Cabo Verde, although it was also pushed to extra time and eventually penalties in its own round of 32 clash with Australia.

The Pharaohs remain unbeaten at the tournament and are looking to make a first-ever quarterfinal, but the team knows perfection will be required to overcome a wily South American adversary. Egypt lost its only previous meeting with Arg"}

{"title": "Ibrahim Hassan announces the absence of two Egyptian national team players from the World Cup match against Argentina", "url": "https://www.voiceofemirates.com/en/sport/2026/07/05/ibrahim-hassan-announces-the-absence-of-two-egyptian-national-team-players-from-the-world-cup-match-against-argentina/", "publishedDate": "2026-07-05T09:34:32.000Z", "author": "Ahmed Khairy", "text": "Ibrahim Hassan announces the absence of two Egyptian national team players from the World Cup match against Argentina

Egypt national team (Photo/Archive)

SHARE

Cairo, Egypt – Ibrahim Hassan, the director of the Egyptian national team, revealed the latest developments regarding the injured players’ condition before the anticipated match against Argentina in the round of 16 of the 2026 World Cup. He confirmed that the coaching staff will only be missing two players due to injury.

The Egyptian national team will face Argentina on Tuesday evening in the round of 16 of the World Cup. In this match, the Pharaohs will be looking to continue their historic run in the tournament.

Ibrahim Hassan said in televised remarks on Sada El Balad channel: “We will not suffer from absences against Argentina except for Ahmed Fatouh and Mohamed Abdel Moneim. Their chances of playing in the match seem difficult due to injury”.

The team manager spoke about Mohamed Salah, praising the Pharaohs’ captain’s performances with the national team. He said, “Mohamed Salah appears in a completely different light when he joins the national team camp, and he delivers outstanding performances under Hossam Hassan"}
